concept.title: Information Typing for AI: Intent Routing and Answer Formatting
concept.shortdesc: How DITA's concept, task, and reference types give AI systems the semantic signal to route user intent and format answers appropriately.

conbody.note: This topic explores how AI systems use DITA's information typing for intent routing and answer formatting. If you are new to information typing, start with Information Typing: Matching Content to Purpose, which covers the authoring fundamentals.

section.title: Why does information typing matter for AI?
section.p: When a user asks a chatbot a question, they have an intent — and different intents require different content types:

row.entry: "What parameters does the HTML5 transform accept?"

section.p: Without information typing, the AI cannot distinguish these content types — it treats everything as undifferentiated text and formats all answers the same way.

section.title: Concept topics: Explaining ideas
section.p: A concept topic answers "what is..." and "why does..." questions. It uses <conbody> for narrative prose, organized into titled <section> elements.
section.codeblock:
Content Reuse in DITA

Content reuse is one of DITA's most powerful features. It allows you to write content once and reference it from multiple locations...

Reuse, Keys, and Variant Management How to write content once and assemble it into multiple deliverables using conref, keyref, and DITAVAL filtering.
Why does content reuse matter?

Content reuse is one of DITA's most powerful features...
section.p: AI benefit: The chatbot knows this is an explanation. It retrieves this topic for "what is..." questions and formats the answer as explanatory prose, not as steps.

section.title: Task topics: Teaching procedures
section.p: A task topic answers "how do I..." questions. DITA tasks enforce a specific structure: <prereq> → <context> → <steps> → <result> → <postreq>.

p.b: Before (unstructured XML) — steps look like any list:

section.codeblock:
Installing DITA-OT

Prerequisites

  • Java 17 or later

Steps

  1. Download from dita-ot.org
  2. Unzip to your preferred location
  3. Add bin/ to your PATH
  4. Verify with dita --version

p.b: After (DITA task) — steps are semantically structured:

section.codeblock:
Installing DITA-OT Download, install, and verify DITA Open Toolkit 4.x on your machine.
  • Java 17 or later — check with java -version
 Download the latest release from dita-ot.org Choose the .zip distribution Unzip to your preferred location You should see a dita-ot-4.x/ directory Add the bin/ directory to your PATH

Verify the installation: dita --version should print the version number.
section.p: AI benefit: The chatbot extracts structured steps. It can present prerequisites separately. It knows the expected result. It formats the answer as a numbered procedure with clear pre/post conditions.

section.title: Reference topics: Lookup information
section.p: A reference topic answers "what are the options for..." questions. It uses <refbody> with tables, property lists, and syntax references.

p.b: Before (unstructured XML) — a table that looks like any table:

section.codeblock: DITA-OT Build Parameters Reference Complete reference of DITA-OT build parameters for the HTML5 and PDF transforms. Parameter Description Default args.input Path to the input DITA map Required ...
section.p: AI benefit: The chatbot knows this is lookup data. It formats the answer as a table or parameterized list. It can extract specific parameter values when users ask about individual parameters.

section.title: How information typing enables intent routing
section.p: With typed topics and the content-intent metadata value, the AI can build an intent-routing pipeline:
This is impossible with unstructured XML — the AI must examine every document equally, regardless of whether it is a concept, task, or reference.
